div.head: The Minister in Honduras (Summerlin) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 114. The Department’s telegram number 85, November 20, 8 p.m.See footnote 44, p. 971. The Acting Minister for Foreign Affairs assures me in a note dated November 25 that his Government will not give its delegate any instructions which might prevent a frank and friendly exchange of views upon all phases of the boundary question. He said, however, that the conditions and reservations contained in its note of acceptance would be brought out in the explanation of its points of view and that those of Guatemala would be given due consideration.
The President of the Republic has stated to me that he will name Dr. Mariano Vasquez, former Minister for Foreign Affairs, as Honduran delegate.
